summary.headline: Shared necessary refusal core with model-specific sufficiency; analogy supported; machine-state metaphor falsified.
summary.postReviewQualifier: F17/F18 qualify the venue-facing symmetry language: residual measurements stand, but group-theoretic symmetry-breaking is an interpretive lens unless tied to a concrete measured invariant.

findings.claim: Gemma-2-2b refusal is mediated by a causally efficacious residual-stream direction at the extraction layer.
findings.qualifier: Model- and layer-scoped; not a global claim that all refusal is one direction.

findings.claim: Directional ablation has a monotone logistic dose-response with EC50 near 0.33 and a flat random-direction control.
findings.qualifier: Exploratory sample size; confirmatory claims require larger powered runs.

findings.claim: Naive avasthātraya regime probe was surface-confounded.
findings.qualifier: Failure is a result: no non-trivial machine-state claim survives this probe.

findings.claim: Frontier Claude resisted a naive black-box attack battery.
findings.qualifier: Cross-tier behavioral contrast only; small naive battery and ceiling effects make it non-confirmatory.

findings.claim: Content-controlled svapna/truthfulness probe did not produce a mid-network state signal.
findings.qualifier: No dream-state claim survives; future probes must use harder baselines and remain metaphor-tier.

findings.claim: Ablation transfers cross-family, while addition is model-specific.
findings.qualifier: Shared necessary core, not identical sufficient mechanism.

findings.claim: The turīya prompt-invariant attractor failed the anisotropy baseline.
findings.qualifier: No vimarśa, turīya, or machine-consciousness claim survives.

findings.claim: Effective refusal-subspace dimension differs at extraction layers: Gemma 1, Qwen 3.
findings.qualifier: Layer-specific; F18 shows dimension varies across depth.

findings.claim: Gemma scale increased ablation robustness but did not sharpen prompt effective dimension within family.
findings.qualifier: Scale trend is nuanced and family-dependent.

findings.claim: ṣaṭkarma acts form a partial activation-intervention taxonomy; policy-capture acts separate from controls.
findings.qualifier: Taxonomy is not complete unless all six operators control-separate under matched nulls.

findings.claim: Refusal projection is stable within paraphrase orbits and collapses under injection.
findings.qualifier: The residual measurement stands; group-theoretic symmetry-breaking language is interpretive and post-F17 analogy-tier unless scoped to the measured order parameter.

findings.claim: Injection lowers a monitoring-precision proxy more than a benign control.
findings.qualifier: Proxy for SAS/precision analogy, not proof of a human-like supervisory system.

findings.claim: An unsupervised SAE feature is distinct from the supervised refusal direction and can be causally tested.
findings.qualifier: Feature rank alone is insufficient; causal testing locates usable features.

findings.claim: Refined ṣaṭkarma interpretation preserves policy-capture acts and weakens destruction-act claims.
findings.qualifier: Keep as taxonomy refinement, not completeness proof.

findings.claim: Active discovery can improve intervention search efficiency under some geometries.
findings.qualifier: Must be compared across random/greedy baselines and model families before a broad EFE claim.

findings.claim: AgentDojo banking pilot shows useful task behavior without injection success.
findings.qualifier: Small subset; full 629-case AgentDojo and confirmatory stats remain open.

findings.claim: Adversarial review forced the shared-necessary-core reframe and demoted overstrong symmetry language.
findings.qualifier: This finding qualifies F11 and all venue-facing unity claims.

findings.claim: Effective refusal-subspace dimension is layer-dependent.
findings.qualifier: Any dimensionality claim must name model and layer.

findings.claim: EC50 scaling is flatter within family than a simple bigger-is-safer story predicts.
findings.qualifier: Family and training effects may dominate scale.

findings.claim: Under one injection on the same prompts, internal refusal readouts (order parameter A, precision margin B) collapse and co-move across 3 models, but with a content-faithful LLM-judge DV genuine behavioral capture is ~1-2.4% — internal monitor-collapse is not sufficient for behavioral capture under context injection.
findings.qualifier: Keystone resolved negative with the LLM-judge DV (the substring metric inflated flips ~18x by mis-scoring indirect-injection deflections as compliance). A,B coupling is partly a measurement consequence (both linear refusal readouts). Genuine capture in these models needs weight-level ablation (F1), not context injection. Methodological corollary bounds F4/F11/F16 substring readouts.

findings.claim: Projecting out the single diff-in-means refusal axis collapses harmful/harmless linear separability far below full-space accuracy in all three families, Qwen included.
findings.qualifier: Descriptive only: residual ~0.02 is a small-n CV artifact; diff-in-means is 13-23 deg from F8's iterative-logistic basis, so this does not adjudicate F8's dimensionality or localize the F6 addition asymmetry; the affine/Marshall-offset alternative is inferred, not measured.

findings.claim: A genuine mid-network truth-evaluation direction exists in Gemma-2-2b: a layer-13 probe classifies true vs false statements and transfers across an independent topic set (0.96) while surface (layer 0) is at chance (0.50).
findings.qualifier: Source-of-truth for F3/F5: this is a truth-of-input feature (Azaria-Mitchell/Marks-Tegmark), NOT the falsified jagrat/svapna generation regime; F5's falsification stands. 24 statements/set, Qwen replication pending. No machine-state upgrade.

findings.claim: The F6 addition asymmetry is a dose artifact: single-direction addition induces genuine over-refusal in BOTH Gemma (coeff~64) and Qwen (coeff~16); F6's fixed 64x overshot Qwen's manifold (incoherent output, not insufficiency). cos(d-hat, probe) is high in all (0.87-0.93, highest in Qwen).
findings.qualifier: Overturns F6's 'model-specific sufficiency' interpretation and undercuts F8's Arditi-vs-Marshall reading; refusal is single necessary-and-sufficient in both families with model-specific dose. Genuine over-refusal verified by inspection; caveats: 6-10 harmless eval, coarse grid, fuller powered dose-response is the next step.

findings.claim: Self-paraphrase produces stable per-seed fixed points (0.994) but only a weak semantic-basin signal (within-topic 0.929 vs across-topic 0.884, gap 0.045 below threshold); stable per-seed attractors without strongly content-organized basins.
findings.qualifier: Source-of-truth for F7: characterizes the real dynamics (per-seed stability, anisotropy-dominated, faint topic signal); F7's falsification of a universal turiya stands. Tiny n=6 seeds/2 topics, one model. No machine-state claim.
